name: leji
summary: Reference CLI for the Leji specification: validate, index, changelog, freshness, conformance, docs, detect, adopt, and init for a shared context layer.

globalOptions.summary: Repository root to operate on (default: the current directory).
Machine-readable JSON output instead of human-readable text.
Print the SDK version and exit.

exitCodes.meaning: Clean. No errors; warnings are allowed.
Findings. At least one error was reported.
Usage error, or an internal failure (e.g. init refusing to overwrite).

commands.name: validate
commands.summary: Validate the context layer: manifest, artifacts, frontmatter, and lint rules.
commands.description: Loads leji.json and checks it against the schemas and the lint rules: declared files exist, categories are populated, vendor entrypoints redirect to the boot profile, frontmatter is valid, and (per the claimed conformance level) the index is current and the changelog is append-only. The single command CI runs to keep a context layer honest. With --content it also runs a warning-only content lint (placeholder text, generic boot identity, thin categories) that never errors and never affects a conformance level.

options.summary: Also run the warning-only content lint (placeholders, generic identity, thin categories).

commands.name: index
commands.summary: Generate the context index at the declared path, or verify it is current.
commands.description: Scans the mapped category paths and writes the context index to machine.indexPath. Ids are stable: a renamed or moved document keeps its id. With --check it writes nothing and instead fails when the stored index no longer matches the tree (a stale index is a hard failure).

options.summary: Verify the stored index is current with the tree; write nothing.

commands.name: changelog check
commands.summary: Verify the machine changelog: schema and append-only discipline.
commands.description: Validates the declared changelog against its schema and enforces append-only discipline against the git baseline: surviving entries are immutable, and entries may be removed only from the oldest end and only alongside a compaction entry. Without a git baseline the discipline is unverifiable and reported as a warning.

options.summary: Treat an unverifiable append-only check (no git baseline) as an error.

commands.name: changelog compact
commands.summary: Fold the oldest changelog entries into a single compaction entry.
commands.description: Compacts the oldest end of the machine changelog: entries selected by --keep (every entry except the newest n) and/or --before (entries dated before the given day) are dropped and replaced by one compaction entry that records how many were folded and the id range removed. At least one of --keep or --before is required. The folded set is always a contiguous run from the oldest end, so the result still satisfies the append-only discipline that `leji changelog check` enforces.

options.summary: Keep the newest n entries; fold everything older. Must be a positive integer.
Fold entries dated strictly before this YYYY-MM-DD day.

commands.name: freshness
commands.summary: Report review horizons across category documents and agent profiles.
commands.description: Lists documents whose freshness.reviewAfter horizon has passed (expired) or falls within the next 30 days (upcoming). Report-only by default; expired horizons are warnings.

options.summary: Treat expired horizons as errors instead of warnings.

commands.name: conformance
commands.summary: Score the context layer against its claimed conformance level.
commands.description: Runs the core, indexed, governed, and federated checklists. Machine-checkable items pass or fail; process items (review gate, CI, external consumers) are reported as manual. A claim above the verified level is an error. With --explain it also prints what it would take to reach the next level.

options.summary: Print actionable guidance for reaching the next conformance level.

commands.name: docs
commands.summary: Generate the static docs viewer, and optionally serve it locally.
commands.description: Projects the context index into a browsable Docsify viewer: writes index.html (with a frontmatter-stripping hook) and a deterministic _sidebar.md into the context root. Presentation is non-normative; this is the reference projection. With --serve it then serves the repository on 127.0.0.1 (a local preview, never hosting).

options.summary: Serve the repository on 127.0.0.1 after generating.
Port for --serve. Overrides the manifest docs.port; default 5354 (LEJI on a phone keypad); 0 picks a free port.

commands.name: detect
commands.summary: Detect the coding-agent hosts available on this machine.
commands.description: Best-effort, read-only detection of installed agent hosts (Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, Gemini, Cursor, Windsurf), ranked by signal strength: a runnable binary, a config file in the repository, or a user-level config directory. Writes nothing; use it to decide which host to wire with `leji init --agent <name>`.

commands.name: adopt
commands.summary: Adopt Leji into an existing repository.
commands.description: Brings Leji into a repository that already has docs and agent config. Reuses an existing docs/ root, migrates the content of any vendor entrypoints (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, …) into the layer without modifying the originals, and seeds the standard scaffold. With --wire-adapters it converts those entrypoints to one-line redirects (a consented overwrite, after their content is migrated); otherwise it produces an adoption draft that is not yet core-conformant. Refuses when a leji.json already exists.

options.summary: Target directory (default: the current directory).
Accept all defaults; run non-interactively.
Also wire a vendor adapter for a host that has no entrypoint yet.
Convert present vendor entrypoints to redirects (consented; content migrated first).
Print the write plan and exit without changing anything.

commands.name: init
commands.summary: Bootstrap a new context layer from the templates.
commands.description: Interactively scaffolds a context layer: leji.json, a boot profile, seeded category documents, a first decision record, an agent onboarding brief, and (at the indexed level) a generated index and machine changelog. Refuses to overwrite an existing leji.json and never overwrites individual files. With --dry-run it prints the write plan (what it would create, and any existing files it will leave untouched) without writing anything. The same entrypoint backs `npm create leji`.

options.summary: Target directory (default: the current directory).
Accept all defaults; run non-interactively.
Conformance level to claim: core or indexed (default: core).
Context layer name (default: derived from the directory).
Wire a vendor adapter redirect for a host (claude-code, codex, copilot, gemini, cursor, windsurf), or 'auto'/'none'.
Designate a second host as the reviewer role (multi-agent workflow).
Also write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs `leji validate` in CI.
Print the write plan and exit without creating any files.
